sources.name: ICAO Annex 15 - Aeronautical Information Services (standard reference)
ICAO Doc 8126 - Aeronautical Information Services Manual
JejuAir AI-Powered NOTAM Analysis System Requirements (customer PDF)

core_entities.name: NOTAM
core_entities.description: Notice to Air Missions - standardized alert about potential hazards or changes affecting flight operations

core_entities.name: RefBookEntry
core_entities.description: Important NOTAM record registered in the REF BOOK by dispatchers for reference

core_entities.name: Flight
core_entities.description: Scheduled flight operation with route and timing information

core_entities.name: Route
core_entities.description: Flight route with waypoints, airways, and spatial coordinates from NAVBLUE data

core_entities.name: Airport
core_entities.description: Airport with ICAO code, facilities, and runway information

core_entities.name: Briefing
core_entities.description: Auto-generated briefing document for dispatchers or crew

core_entities.name: Dispatcher
core_entities.description: Flight dispatcher user who monitors NOTAMs and manages flight operations

domain_workflows.name: NOTAM Intake & Triage
domain_workflows.description: NOTAM received through AFTN is parsed, AI-scored for importance, and classified for dispatcher attention

domain_workflows.name: NOTAM Impact Analysis
domain_workflows.description: Analyzed NOTAM is cross-referenced against airline routes and schedule to identify affected flights

domain_workflows.name: REF BOOK Registration
domain_workflows.description: Important NOTAMs are registered in the REF BOOK by dispatcher with AI assistance

domain_workflows.name: Briefing Generation
domain_workflows.description: AI generates briefing documents (Company NOTAM, DISP COMMENT) for dispatcher review and crew distribution

domain_workflows.name: Route Deviation Decision
domain_workflows.description: When a NOTAM significantly impacts a route, AI suggests alternatives and dispatcher makes the final decision

kpis.name: NOTAM Processing Time
kpis.formula: Time from NOTAM receipt to classification completion
kpis.typical_target: < 5 minutes for routine, < 1 minute for urgent
kpis.relevance: Primary efficiency metric — shows AI automation value vs manual processing (currently 15-30 min per NOTAM)

kpis.name: Critical NOTAM Detection Rate
kpis.formula: AI-detected critical NOTAMs / Total actual critical NOTAMs x 100
kpis.relevance: Safety-critical metric — AI must not miss operationally significant NOTAMs

kpis.name: False Positive Rate
kpis.formula: Non-critical NOTAMs flagged as critical / Total flagged as critical x 100
kpis.relevance: Efficiency metric — too many false positives cause alert fatigue

kpis.name: Daily NOTAM Volume
kpis.relevance: Dashboard widget showing system load and filtering effectiveness

kpis.name: Filtered vs Total Ratio
kpis.formula: NOTAMs requiring dispatcher attention / Total received x 100
kpis.relevance: Shows automation effectiveness — lower ratio means better filtering

kpis.name: REF BOOK Registration Accuracy
kpis.formula: AI-suggested REF BOOK entries accepted by dispatcher / Total AI suggestions x 100
kpis.relevance: Measures AI suggestion quality for REF BOOK registration

kpis.name: Briefing Generation Time
kpis.formula: Time from request to complete briefing package
kpis.relevance: Key time-saving metric for crew briefing preparation

kpis.name: Route Impact Coverage
kpis.formula: Routes with NOTAM impact analysis / Total active routes x 100
kpis.relevance: Ensures no route is missed in impact analysis

competitor_features.feature: NOTAM text parsing and structured extraction
competitor_features.prevalence: Standard in all NOTAM management tools (Jeppesen, NAVBLUE, SITA)
competitor_features.suggestion: Parse ICAO format into structured fields for search and filtering

competitor_features.prevalence: Common in modern NOTAM tools — plot NOTAM areas on map with route overlay
competitor_features.suggestion: Interactive map showing NOTAM circles/polygons overlaid on airline routes

competitor_features.feature: NOTAM change tracking and diff view
competitor_features.prevalence: Available in Jeppesen and NAVBLUE — track NOTAM replacements and cancellations
competitor_features.suggestion: Show NOTAM history with highlighted changes when NOTAMR replaces existing NOTAM

competitor_features.prevalence: Standard feature — auto-archive expired NOTAMs and alert on soon-to-expire
competitor_features.suggestion: Auto-manage NOTAM lifecycle with expiry countdown and archive

competitor_features.prevalence: Common — dispatchers save filter presets by route, airport, or NOTAM type
competitor_features.suggestion: Allow dispatchers to save and share filter profiles for their regular routes

competitor_features.prevalence: Emerging AI feature — convert cryptic NOTAM text to readable summaries
competitor_features.suggestion: LLM generates human-readable summary alongside raw NOTAM text

competitor_features.prevalence: Common in 24/7 operations — auto-generate summary of active NOTAMs for incoming shift
competitor_features.suggestion: Generate shift handover briefing with new/changed NOTAMs since last shift start

competitor_features.prevalence: Available in enterprise tools — historical analysis of NOTAM patterns
competitor_features.suggestion: Show NOTAM volume trends, most-affected airports/routes over time
Real-time alerts for NOTAMs exceeding importance threshold

terminology.NOTAM: Notice to Air Missions — official notice of aviation hazards, restrictions, or changes affecting flight operations
terminology.Q-Code: Qualifier code in NOTAM Q-line — 5-letter code (e.g., QMRLC) encoding subject and condition. 2nd/3rd letters = subject, 4th/5th letters = condition
terminology.AFTN: Aeronautical Fixed Telecommunication Network — global network for exchanging aviation messages including NOTAMs
terminology.FIR: Flight Information Region — defined airspace area where flight information and alerting services are provided
terminology.ICAO: International Civil Aviation Organization — UN agency setting international aviation standards
terminology.IATA: International Air Transport Association — airline industry trade association
terminology.REF BOOK: Reference Book — Jeju Air internal record of operationally important NOTAMs maintained by dispatchers
terminology.Company NOTAM: Airline-specific NOTAM briefing document prepared for crew, containing operationally relevant information
terminology.DISP COMMENT: Dispatcher Comment — dispatcher's operational notes and recommendations added to crew briefing
terminology.J-FOS: Jeju Air Flight Operations System — Jeju Air's internal flight operations management system
terminology.NAVBLUE: Airbus subsidiary providing route planning and navigation data solutions to airlines
terminology.NOTAMN: New NOTAM — creates a new notice
terminology.NOTAMR: Replacement NOTAM — replaces an existing NOTAM
terminology.NOTAMC: Cancellation NOTAM — cancels an existing NOTAM
terminology.Q-line: Qualifier line in NOTAM format containing FIR, Q-code, traffic type, purpose, scope, coordinates, and radius
terminology.Series: NOTAM series identifier (A-Z) grouping NOTAMs by geographic or functional category
terminology.SFC: Surface — lowest altitude reference in NOTAM (Field F)
terminology.UNL: Unlimited — highest altitude reference in NOTAM (Field G)
terminology.UTC: Coordinated Universal Time — standard time reference for all aviation operations (also called Zulu time)
terminology.Waypoint: Named geographic point used to define flight routes (identified by 5-letter ICAO code)
terminology.Flight Level: Altitude reference based on standard pressure (e.g., FL350 = 35,000 feet)
terminology.AIP: Aeronautical Information Publication — official publication containing permanent aeronautical information
terminology.PIB: Pre-flight Information Bulletin — briefing package of active NOTAMs relevant to a planned flight
terminology.SNOWTAM: Special NOTAM series reporting runway snow/ice conditions
terminology.ASHTAM: Special NOTAM series reporting volcanic ash activity
terminology.TCO: Total Cost of Operations — used in LCC context for route profitability analysis

regulations.name: ICAO Annex 15 - Aeronautical Information Services
regulations.description: Defines NOTAM format, distribution requirements, and aeronautical information management standards
regulations.relevance: NOTAM format compliance is mandatory; the system must parse and display NOTAMs conforming to this standard

regulations.name: ICAO Doc 8126 - Aeronautical Information Services Manual
regulations.description: Detailed guidance on NOTAM origination, processing, and distribution procedures
regulations.relevance: Reference for understanding NOTAM lifecycle and processing requirements

regulations.name: ICAO Doc 8400 - ICAO Abbreviations and Codes
regulations.description: Standard aviation abbreviations and Q-codes used in NOTAMs
regulations.relevance: Required reference for Q-code decoding and NOTAM abbreviation interpretation

regulations.name: Korean Aviation Safety Act (항공안전법)
regulations.description: Korean national regulation governing aviation safety including NOTAM management requirements
regulations.relevance: Korean regulatory context for Jeju Air operations

regulations.name: MOLIT Aviation Information Management Standards (국토교통부 항공정보관리 기준)
regulations.description: Korean Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport standards for aeronautical information
regulations.relevance: Governs NOTAM handling requirements for Korean airlines

suggested_requirements.title: NOTAM change tracking (NOTAMR/NOTAMC diff view)
suggested_requirements.reason: Industry standard feature — when a NOTAM is replaced or cancelled, showing the diff helps dispatchers quickly understand what changed

suggested_requirements.title: Shift handover report generation
suggested_requirements.reason: With 3 dispatchers on 24h rotation, auto-generated shift handover reports listing new/changed/critical NOTAMs since last shift are essential for continuity

suggested_requirements.title: NOTAM expiry countdown and auto-archive
suggested_requirements.reason: Standard lifecycle management — expired NOTAMs should auto-archive and NOTAMs expiring within a configurable window should be flagged

suggested_requirements.title: Custom filter profiles per dispatcher
suggested_requirements.reason: Dispatchers typically monitor specific route groups — saved filter profiles reduce setup time at shift start

suggested_requirements.title: NOTAM plain-language summary
suggested_requirements.reason: LLM capability already present — generating a human-readable Korean translation of cryptic NOTAM text is a high-value low-effort addition

suggested_requirements.title: Critical NOTAM push notifications
suggested_requirements.reason: Real-time alerts for NOTAMs exceeding importance threshold ensure immediate dispatcher attention even during multitasking

suggested_requirements.title: NOTAM volume and trend analytics dashboard
suggested_requirements.reason: Historical NOTAM pattern analysis helps resource planning and identifies recurring issues at specific airports/routes

suggested_requirements.title: Audit trail for dispatcher actions
suggested_requirements.reason: Aviation regulatory compliance requires traceability of operational decisions — log who reviewed, approved, and acted on each NOTAM

common_enums: NotamStatus: new | active | analyzed | ref-book-registered | expired | cancelled | replaced
ImportanceLevel: critical | high | medium | low | routine
QCodeSubject: MA(aerodrome) | MR(runway) | MX(taxiway) | NA(enroute-navaid) | FA(airspace) | ...
NotamPurpose: N(immediate) | B(PIB) | O(flight-ops) | M(misc) | K(checklist)
FlightStatus: scheduled | dispatched | in-flight | arrived | cancelled | diverted

notam_format_structure.field_c: C) YYMMDDHHmm — effective to (UTC) or PERM
notam_format_structure.field_e: E) Plain text description with ICAO abbreviations
notam_format_structure.field_f: F) Lower altitude limit (e.g., SFC, FL050)
notam_format_structure.field_g: G) Upper altitude limit (e.g., FL350, UNL)
notam_format_structure.coordinates: Latitude/Longitude in NOTAM format: DDMMssN/DDDMMssE (degrees, minutes, seconds)
notam_format_structure.radius: Radius in nautical miles from coordinates
